name: Kelly Johnson
technical_background: No formal coding experience. Strong writing, editing, and content strategy skills from agency work. Daily user of multiple GenAI tools for content creation but approaches are intuitive and ad hoc rather than systematic. Comfortable with CMS platforms and basic SEO tools.

current_profile.summary: Content writer at a digital marketing agency with 3 years of experience creating blog posts, social media content, email campaigns, and website copy for diverse B2B and B2C clients. Strong writing and editing skills with attention to brand voice consistency. Uses ChatGPT and Claude daily for brainstorming, drafting, and rewriting content. Actively uses Midjourney and DALL-E for visual content concepts and social media graphics. Comfortable with CMS platforms (WordPress, HubSpot), basic SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush), and content analytics. Prompt work is intuitive — writes effective prompts through trial and error but has no systematic framework for prompt design, testing, or iteration. No coding background. Has experimented with chaining prompts for longer content workflows but informally.
technical_skills: Brand voice and style guide development
current_profile.ai_experience: Daily user of ChatGPT and Claude for content drafting, brainstorming, rewriting, and research. Active user of Midjourney and DALL-E for visual content creation and concept exploration. Has experimented with prompt chaining for longer content workflows (research → outline → draft → edit) but approaches are informal and not documented. Comfortable getting good outputs through trial and error but lacks systematic frameworks for prompt design, evaluation, or version control. Has not worked with APIs, structured outputs, or production prompt systems.

learning_intent: Wants to future-proof career by becoming a prompt/AI workflow specialist who designs repeatable, high-quality content pipelines with GenAI. Goal: move from intuitive prompting to systematic prompt engineering — learn structured prompt design, evaluation criteria for AI content quality, prompt library management, and collaboration with product/engineering teams to integrate prompts into production tools. Wants to bridge the gap between creative content strategy and technical prompt engineering.

target_jd.title: Prompt Engineer / AI Content Strategist
requirements: Design prompt templates and workflows for consistent, high-quality AI-generated content across formats
Iterate prompts using structured feedback, evaluation criteria, and A/B testing; maintain prompt libraries
Collaborate with product and engineering teams to integrate prompts into tools and platforms
Ensure ethical and brand-safe content generation; manage hallucination and factual accuracy risks
Build and maintain prompt style guides with version control and documentation standards
Design evaluation rubrics for AI-generated content quality (accuracy, tone, brand voice, engagement)
Create few-shot example libraries and reference datasets for consistent prompt behavior
Develop prompt debugging and optimization workflows for production content systems
Train content teams on AI-augmented writing workflows and best practices
Monitor AI content pipeline performance and iterate based on quality metrics and user feedback

expected_skill_gaps.description: Understanding model limitations (hallucinations, knowledge cutoffs), training data concepts, IP/copyright basics for AI-generated content.
Systematic prompting: instructions with constraints, few-shot examples, prompt debugging, role/persona prompting, and structured JSON/schema outputs.
Evaluating AI content quality: rubric-based evaluation, LLM-as-judge patterns, prompt unit tests, and A/B testing for prompt variants.
Content safety basics: AI security awareness, data leakage prevention in content generation, and brand-risk guardrails.
AI governance basics for content generation: responsible use guidelines, copyright considerations, and content generation ethics.
Understanding multimodal AI more deeply: image generation quality assurance, document understanding, and cross-modal content workflows.
Writing AI workflow documentation, creating prompt style guides, and training others on AI content creation best practices.
Building systematic personal AI workflows, evaluating new AI tools, and teaching AI skills to content teams.
